question_text: Write any four important characteristics of Object Oriented Programming. Give example of any one using C++.

question_text: Write class CITY in C++ with private: Ccode CName Pop KM Density DenCal(); public: Record() View() (shows Highly Populated City if Density>10000)
answer_text: class CITY{int Ccode; char CName[20]; long int Pop; float KM Density; void DenCal(); public: void Record(); void View();}; void CITY::DenCal(){Density=Pop/KM;} void CITY::Record(){cin>>Ccode; gets(CName); cin>>Pop>>KM; DenCal();} void CITY::View(){cout<<Ccode<<CName<<Pop<<KM<<Density; if(Density>10000)cout<<"Highly Populated City";}

question_text: Write FixSalary(float Salary[] int N) to modify: <100000 add 35% >=100000 and <200000 add 30% >=200000 add 20%
answer_text: void FixSalary(float Salary[] int N){for(int i=0;i<N;i++){if(Salary[i]<100000) Salary[i]+=0.35*Salary[i]; else if(Salary[i]<200000) Salary[i]+=0.3*Salary[i]; else Salary[i]+=0.20*Salary[i];}}

question_text: Write DISP3CHAR() to read KIDINME.TXT and display all words with exactly 3 characters.
answer_text: void DISP3CHAR(){ifstream Fil; Fil.open("KIDINME.TXT"); char W[20]; Fil>>W; while(!Fil.eof()){if(strlen(W)==3) cout<<W<<" "; Fil>>W;} Fil.close();}

question_text: Write ONOFFER() to read binary file TOYS.DAT and display toys with Status=ON OFFER.
answer_text: void ONOFFER(){TOYS T; ifstream fin; fin.open("TOYS.DAT" ios::binary); while(fin.read((char*)&T sizeof(T))){if(strcmp(T.SeeOffer() "ON OFFER")==0) T.View();} fin.close();}

question_text: What is difference between Multilevel and Multiple inheritance? Give examples for both.
answer_text: Multilevel: chain A->B->C. Multiple: class C(A B) two parents. Example Multilevel: class C(B) where class B(A). Example Multiple: class C(A B).

question_text: Output of try-except code with inputs AMAR THREE A123 1200? Explain try and except used.
answer_text: Prompts Re-enter integer 3 times then prints 6. try attempts int conversion; except ValueError handles non-integer input and increments Start by 2.

question_text: Write class CITY in Python with Ccode CName Pop KM Density and DenCal() Record() View() methods.
How to implement abstract method in Python? Give example.
answer_text: Abstract method: unimplemented method in base class. Derived class defines it or raises NotImplementedError. Example: class Shape: def findArea(self): pass; class Square(Shape): def findArea(self): return self.side*self.side

question_text: What is significance of super() method? Give example.
answer_text: super() calls base class methods from derived class. Example: class GradStudent(Student): def __init__(self): super(GradStudent self).__init__(); self.subject=""

question_text: Show list after First Second Third pass of Selection Sort (descending): 12 14 -54 64 90 24
Write Binary Search method for descending-order list. Return position or -1 if not found.
answer_text: def binarysrch(nums x): high=len(nums); low=0; while low<high: mid=(low+high)//2; midval=nums[mid]; if midval>x: low=mid+1; elif midval<x: high=mid; else: return mid; return -1

question_text: Write Insert(City) and Delete(City) methods in Python for Queue data structure.
answer_text: class queue: city=[]; def Insert(self): a=input(); queue.city.append(a); def Delete(self): if queue.city==[]: print("Queue empty"); else: print(queue.city[0]); queue.city.pop(0)

question_text: Write method to find and display prime numbers between 2 to N. Pass N as argument.
answer_text: def prime(N): for a in range(2 N): Prime=1; for I in range(2 a): if a%I==0: Prime=0; if Prime==1: print(a)

question_text: Write Python method to write multiple lines of text into text file daynote.txt
answer_text: def writel(): f=open("daynote.txt" "w"); while True: line=input(); f.write(line); if input("More?")=="N": break; f.close()

question_text: Write method to search pickled file emp.dat and display record where Empno matches A0005.
answer_text: def search(): f=open("emp.dat" "rb"); try: while True: e=pickle.load(f); if e.Empno=="A0005": e.Display(); except EOFError: pass; f.close()

question_text: Differentiate between PAN and LAN types of networks.
answer_text: PAN: Personal Area Network around individual person. LAN: Local Area Network within confined area up to 1 km.

question_text: Which protocol helps transfer files to and from a remote computer?

question_text: Write two advantages of 3G over 2G in terms of speed and services.
answer_text: Speed: Faster web browsing faster file transfer. Services: Better video clarity better security.

question_text: Write two characteristics of Web 2.0.
answer_text: 1. Interactive through social media. 2. Easy online information exchange and collaboration.

question_text: What is the basic difference between Computer Worm and Trojan Horse?
answer_text: Trojan Horse: disguised malware user installs voluntarily. Computer Worm: self-replicating program spreading through network without user action.

question_text: Categorise JavaScript ASP VBScript JSP under Client side and Server Side scripts.

question_text: Which service/protocol enables live interaction between Head Office experts and YHUB?
